title: Structs, Methods, Method Sets, Embedding, and Composition · Program Defense
summary: A job scheduler needs explicit domain records, validated transitions, method behavior, and composition without accidental promotion. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a validated job-domain model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design structs, field visibility, literals, zero states, comparison, and tags as explicit data contracts.
Choose value or pointer receivers from mutation, copying, nil behavior, consistency, and method-set evidence.
Predict method sets, implicit address-taking, interface satisfaction, and behavior of named pointer and value types.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
